channel.title: Look Up, Kansas City — Blog
channel.description: Notes, observations, and essays on Kansas City — the ongoing notebook alongside the reference site.

item.title: On Honest History
item.description: Why this site holds the restrictive covenants alongside the beautiful neighborhoods, and the urban-renewal demolitions alongside the Art Deco skyline. A note on method — and an argument against sequestering the hard material.

item.title: What the World Cup Tells Us About Kansas City
item.description: The 2026 tournament as the current instance of Kansas City's recurring impulse to imagine itself larger. What gets built in the reach — and what happens after the attention leaves.

item.title: A Walk Through the Crossroads at Midnight
item.description: After the First Friday crowds leave, the warehouses fall back into their older quiet. Brick, streetlight, and the illuminated crown of Power & Light visible from eight blocks south.

item.title: Watching the McConahay Building Come Back
item.description: The second-floor office at 31st and Forest, where Walt Disney ran a cartoon studio into bankruptcy in 1923, is coming back as a preserved cultural site. What the restoration does and does not do.

item.title: The Power & Light Building at Dusk
item.description: Standing at 14th and Baltimore at golden hour, watching the crown illumination come on. What KC's architectural ambition feels like when you stand inside its physical reality.

item.title: Welcome to the Notebook
item.description: A short note on what the blog is, what it isn't, and why it exists separate from the reference site. The encyclopedia and the notebook do different work.
